title: oxalis rugeliana
intro: Oxalis rugeliana, also known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant is rich in medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and urinary problems. The health benefits of Oxalis rugeliana include its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Oxalis rugeliana can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
